summary: Phase 1 backend + frontend testing for SmartClix. 11/12 backend tests passed (brute-force lockout fails in multi-pod env). 11/11 frontend Playwright flows passed: login, forgot-password, error-state, admin dashboard, role-based sidebar, admin-only Team/Email settings tabs, operator invite, logout, unauth redirect, reviewer restricted nav & settings.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout broken behind k8s ingress. Backend uses request.client.host which is the ingress controller pod IP (e.g., 10.208.130.77 vs 10.208.130.78). Each backend pod sees a different identifier, so the per-identifier counter is split across pods and the 5-attempt threshold is never reached. Verified by inspecting db.login_attempts: same email produced two identifiers (10.208.130.77:<email> count=1 and 10.208.130.78:<email> count=0 + locked_until). The 6th login attempt returned 401 instead of 429.

action_items: Fix brute-force IP detection: parse X-Forwarded-For (or X-Real-IP) header in /api/auth/login instead of using request.client.host. Suggested code: ip = (request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') or '').split(',')[0].strip() or (request.client.host if request.client else 'unknown'). After fix, re-run backend tests to confirm 429 lockout.
critical_code_review_comments: request.client.host is unreliable behind proxies/ingress; always honor X-Forwarded-For for security-sensitive identifiers (rate limit, lockout, audit).

Brute-force counter reset to 0 once lockout begins (register_failure sets count=0 with locked_until). Works correctly per-identifier, but consider also locking by email-only as a second key to mitigate distributed attackers (defense-in-depth).
Cookies are set with SameSite=None+Secure which is correct for cross-origin preview; ensure production deploys keep HTTPS.
ObjectId is correctly serialized via serialize_operator (no _id leak); good.
Operators DELETE: prevents self-delete, but does NOT prevent deleting the last admin — could lock out the org. Recommend: if role==admin and admin_count==1, refuse delete/role-change.
PATCH /operators/{id}: no protection against demoting the last admin (same risk).
No rate limiting on /api/auth/forgot-password — abuser could flood the endpoint; consider per-IP throttle.

Password reset token TTL index uses 'expires_at' with expireAfterSeconds=0 but the field is stored as a Python datetime via insert_one — verify MongoDB sees it as BSON Date (not string) so the TTL actually expires it; spot-check OK.
VALID_ROLES check in create_operator runs after Pydantic validation; consider using Literal type for stricter validation upfront.
seed_data_creation: Cleaned up TEST_-prefixed operators (test_inv_*, test_mgr_*) and login_attempts entries created during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/. Run: cd /app/backend/tests && pytest -v. The only failing test (TestBruteForce::test_lockout_after_5_failures) will pass once main agent fixes the X-Forwarded-For client-IP parsing in /api/auth/login. If the test still fails post-fix, manually clear login_attempts via: mongosh smartclix --eval 'db.login_attempts.deleteMany({})'. Frontend cookie-based auth is working end-to-end via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: Multiple backend replicas behind a kubernetes service receive traffic from different ingress controller pods. FastAPI's request.client.host reports the immediate upstream (ingress pod IP, e.g., 10.208.130.77/.78), which differs between requests. The brute-force protection keys login_attempts by `<client_ip>:<email>`, so the same logical user appears as two separate identifiers depending on which ingress pod proxied the request. Counter never accumulates to 5 on a single key. REPRO: Loop 5x POST /api/auth/login with fixed bad email — observe two docs in db.login_attempts with different ip prefixes and counts splitting. MITIGATION: Use the client IP from X-Forwarded-For header (first IP in the list) for the identifier; fall back to request.client.host only if header absent.
